name: Haselnusstorte (Hazelnut Torte)
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the egg yolks and sugar together until very light, about 5 minutes.
2. Gradually add the water while continuing to beat the mixture.
3. Sift the flour and baking powder together, then mix with 1 cup of ground hazelnuts.
4. Gently fold the flour mixture into the beaten yolks.
5. Beat the egg whites until soft peaks form.
6. Carefully fold the beaten whites into the batter.
7. Pour the batter into a greased and floured 10-inch springform pan.
8. Bake at 375 degrees F for 30 minutes or until the cake is done.
9. Allow the cake to cool on a wire rack.
10. Once completely cooled, split the cake into two layers.
11. Fold the vanilla extract, confectioners' sugar, and remaining 1/2 cup of ground hazelnuts into the whipped cream.
12. Spread the whipped cream between the two cake layers and on top of the cake.
13. Chill until serving time.
14. Garnish with fresh strawberries, if desired.  Hazelnuts are available at most stores under the name Filberts. They should be blanched by boiling for 5 minutes, then removing the skins when cool enough to handle. To grind, place about 1/4 cup at a time in a blender or chop as finely as possible with a sharp knife.
